articles.title: Hospitals in Cebu on heightened alert for health effects tied to haze
articles.description: CEBU CITY, Philippines – Although no one has been hospitalized due to the haze so far, hospitals in Metro Cebu have been urged to prepare. These were among the measures the provincial government has started implementing as Cebu Province’s urban center remains blanketed with smog for a third straight day on Monday, April 20. Gov. Pamela Baricuatro instructed that Capitol-run hospitals, particularly in Metro Cebu, be placed on heightened alert, said Ainjeliz Dela Torre-Orong, provincial information officer (PIO). READ MORE: Haze: What health risks does it pose? Capitol’s emergency operations center now runs 24/7 “While there are no reports (of [...]... articles.title: Dangerous heat index grips 3 areas on Monday
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — Three areas experienced a dangerous heat index on Monday, according tothe state weather bureau. The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical, and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) reported that as of 5 p.m., Sangley Point, Cavite logged the highest peak heat index for the day at 43oC. This marked the second consecutive day Sangley Point recorded a danger-level heat index after peaking at 44oC on Sunday. Meanwhile, Baler, Aurora and Iloilo City both had a heat index of 42oC. READ: Here’s how to protect yourself from high heat index, according to DOH Heat indices ranging from 42oC to 51oC are under [...]... Keep on reading: Dangerous heat index grips 3 areas on Monday

articles.title: World: 2025 Disasters in Numbers
articles.description: Countries: World, Afghanistan, Bolivia (Plurinational State of), Indonesia, Myanmar, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines, Somalia, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Syrian Arab Republic, Thailand Source: Centre for Research on the Epidemiology of Disasters Please refer to the attached file. In 2025,1 the Emergency Events Database (EM-DAT) recorded 358 natural hazard-related disasters.2 These events resulted in 16,607 fatalities, affected 110.2 million people and caused US$169.68B in economic losses. Asia accounted for a disproportionately large share of disaster-related deaths, due largely to major earthquakes in Myanmar and Afghanistan. The year was also marked by a record-breaking drought in Syria, which left 80% of the population in need of assistance; and by the Palisades and Eaton Fires, which ranked among the costliest wildfires ever recorded, both globally and in the United States of America.

articles.title: Country diary: Even in tame horses, a wildness remains | Kate Blincoe
articles.description: Caistor St Edmund, Norfolk: A large mare is being aggressive not just to the dying deer in its paddock, but also me for trying to save it It’s been a strange few weeks, not least because Dad is in hospital. On the farm, we’re all trying to carry on as normal. Outside, too, has seemed peculiar: three oystercatchers on the cut grass of the Norfolk Showground event venue, their orange beaks flashing neon as they probed for earthworms. Unlike many waders, oystercatchers can nest in peculiar places such as rooftops or roundabouts , as extra protection for their young. Still, to find them here, hemmed in by a dual carriageway, is a surprise. Dad reminds me, from his hospital bed, that the River Yare is close. Continue reading...
